name: stripe-handler
description: Handle Stripe payments, custom checkouts, and webhook fulfillment outside of standard plans/credits.
capabilities: Outline a custom checkout session flow for one time products
Describe webhook routing using metadata types
Explain how to offload fulfillment to Inngest background jobs
List required Stripe environment variables for keys and webhook secrets
Provide local webhook testing steps with Stripe CLI
author: AayushBaniya2006
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged command execution and environment access patterns in documentation. Review shows Stripe CLI examples and standard environment variable guidance with no executable code. No evidence of data exfiltration or malicious intent was found.
valueStatement: Custom payment flows for one time products are complex to implement safely. This skill provides patterns for Stripe checkout sessions, webhook handling, and background processing with Inngest.
limitations: Does not generate UI components or client side Stripe elements
Requires a Stripe account and valid API keys
Does not replace official Stripe API documentation
Assumes a Next.js style backend and may need adaptation for other stacks

useCases.title: Launch a one time product checkout
useCases.description: Create a server side checkout flow for a digital product with metadata driven fulfillment.

useCases.title: Route custom webhook events
useCases.description: Add metadata based routing to separate custom purchases from plan or credit flows.

useCases.targetUser: Platform teams handling high volume payments
useCases.title: Scale fulfillment with background jobs
useCases.description: Move slow fulfillment to Inngest to avoid webhook timeouts and ensure idempotent processing.

promptTemplates.title: Basic checkout session
promptTemplates.prompt: Draft a server side Stripe checkout session for a one time $49 product. Include metadata type and productId.

promptTemplates.title: Webhook routing logic
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain how to route checkout.session.completed events using metadata.type and where to place the logic.

promptTemplates.title: Background processing plan
promptTemplates.prompt: Design an Inngest workflow for payment fulfillment with idempotency checks and a confirmation email step.

promptTemplates.title: Environment setup checklist
promptTemplates.prompt: List the Stripe environment variables needed for server, client, and webhook verification, and describe where they are used.

bestPractices: Use metadata.type to separate custom products from plan and credit flows.
Respond to webhooks quickly and offload slow work to Inngest.
Add idempotency checks before creating orders or granting access.
antiPatterns: Relying only on product IDs for routing when metadata is available.
Running long database updates directly inside the webhook handler.
Skipping webhook signature verification with STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET.

faq.question: When should I use stripe-handler?
faq.answer: Use it for one time products or custom services that are not standard plans or credit packages.

faq.question: Does this skill create Stripe products for me?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on checkout, webhooks, and fulfillment logic, not product management in Stripe.

faq.question: How do I prevent duplicate fulfillment?
faq.answer: Store the Stripe session ID and check for an existing order before fulfilling.

faq.question: What environment variables are required?
faq.answer: You need STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, and NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY.

faq.question: Can I use it with subscriptions?
faq.answer: Yes, but it is optimized for custom flows. Use plans-handler for standard subscription plans.

faq.question: Is Inngest required?
faq.answer: No. It is recommended for long running fulfillment and high volume events.
